summary: SKXamlCanvas.Skia.cs throws NullReferenceException when the canvas resizes because CreateBitmap() calls FreeBitmap() twice, nullifying the bitmap that was just recreated between the two calls.

stepsToReproduce: Use SKXamlCanvas in an Uno Platform Skia project
Trigger a canvas size change (e.g., window resize)
NullReferenceException is thrown at line 72 of SKXamlCanvas.Skia.cs
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Uno Platform with Skia backend, SkiaSharp 3.116.0

bugSignals.errorMessage: NullReferenceException on bitmap.PixelBuffer.AsStream() after FreeBitmap() nullifies bitmap field

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The code path in CreateBitmap() has not changed since the last modification (commit c264c449b, July 2024, which only changed color type to Bgra8888). The double-FreeBitmap bug has been present since the file was created.

analysis.summary: The CreateBitmap() method in SKXamlCanvas.Skia.cs calls FreeBitmap() twice when the canvas size changes: once at line 87 (bitmap size mismatch) and once at line 106 (pixels null check). The second FreeBitmap() call nullifies the bitmap that was just re-created in lines 89-102, causing a NRE when DoInvalidate() accesses bitmap.PixelBuffer at line 72. The WinUI version of SKXamlCanvas does not have this issue because it allocates bitmap and pixels together in a single conditional block.
analysis.rationale: Reporter (MartinZikmund, Uno Platform contributor) correctly identified the root cause in the source code. Code investigation confirms: FreeBitmap() sets both bitmap and pixels to null, and when called a second time after bitmap recreation, it re-nullifies bitmap while only pixels gets recreated. This is clearly a bug — not by-design behavior. Classified as high severity because any canvas resize triggers the NRE.

keySignals.text: This line of code may throw NRE if the canvas changed its size, because the FreeBitmap method may have reset bitmap field to null
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter correctly identified the double-FreeBitmap race condition in CreateBitmap().
Reporter indicates the previous major version worked, though this specific code path existed since the Uno Skia file was created.

codeInvestigation.finding: First FreeBitmap() call when bitmap size mismatches — sets bitmap=null AND pixels=null
Bitmap is re-created when null and dimensions are valid — but this new bitmap is about to be destroyed
Second FreeBitmap() call because pixels is still null from step 1 — this nullifies the bitmap just created in lines 89-102
bitmap.PixelBuffer.AsStream() throws NRE because bitmap is null after the double FreeBitmap()
WinUI CreateBitmap() has a single FreeBitmap() call and allocates bitmap+pixels together — does not have this bug
FreeBitmap() unconditionally sets bitmap=null when pixels!=null, even if bitmap was just recreated

resolution.hypothesis: CreateBitmap() has a logic error: it manages bitmap and pixel allocation in separate conditional blocks, but FreeBitmap() clears both. When pixels need reallocation, the second FreeBitmap() destroys the just-created bitmap.

proposals.title: Restructure CreateBitmap to allocate bitmap and pixels together
proposals.description: Refactor CreateBitmap() to match the WinUI pattern: a single FreeBitmap() call followed by a single conditional block that allocates both bitmap and pixels together. Remove the second FreeBitmap() call at line 106 and merge the pixel allocation into the bitmap creation block.

proposals.title: Remove bitmap nullification from second FreeBitmap call path
proposals.description: Instead of calling FreeBitmap() at line 106 (which also nulls bitmap), only free the pixel handle and reset pixel-related fields without touching bitmap. This is a minimal fix but leaves the asymmetric allocation structure in place.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Restructure CreateBitmap to allocate bitmap and pixels together
resolution.recommendedReason: Matches the WinUI pattern which is proven to work, eliminates the structural issue rather than patching around it, and is still a small change.

actionability.reason: Root cause is clearly identified in the code, but the fix needs testing on Uno Platform Skia targets. The reporter is a known Uno contributor (MartinZikmund) and may contribute a fix.

actions.description: Acknowledge the bug report with root cause confirmation and fix direction
actions.comment:
Thanks for the precise report — the analysis is correct.

The root cause is a double `FreeBitmap()` call in `CreateBitmap()`. When the canvas resizes:
1. Line 87: `FreeBitmap()` nulls both `bitmap` and `pixels`
2. Lines 89-102: `bitmap` is re-created
3. Line 106: `FreeBitmap()` is called again (because `pixels` is still null), which nulls `bitmap` a second time
4. Lines 108-113: Only `pixels` is re-created — `bitmap` stays null
5. Line 72: `bitmap.PixelBuffer` throws NRE

The WinUI `SKXamlCanvas.cs` doesn't have this issue because it allocates bitmap and pixels together in a single block. The fix would be to restructure `CreateBitmap()` to follow the same pattern.

Would you like to submit a PR for this?
